items.question: Who relies on Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Categorical, and Gaussian models in ML pipelines?
items.answer: Data scientists and ML engineers choose these distributions when designing classifiers, count models, regression objectives, and generative components—anywhere likelihoods or cross-entropy tie predictions to data types.

items.question: What is the difference between a PMF and a PDF?
items.answer: A PMF gives actual probabilities P(X=x) for discrete X and sums to 1; a PDF gives density f(x) for continuous X where probabilities come from integrals over intervals and point masses are zero.

items.question: When should you use Poisson instead of Binomial?
items.answer: Use Binomial for a fixed number n of yes/no trials with constant p; use Poisson for open-ended event counts in an interval when events are rare and independent with rate λ, especially when n is large and p is small.

items.question: Where do Categorical distributions appear in neural networks?
items.answer: At the output softmax layer: logits map to class probabilities π_k that parameterize a Categorical law over labels, trained with categorical cross-entropy (negative log-likelihood).

items.question: Why assume Gaussian noise in linear regression?
items.answer: Gaussian errors make squared loss the maximum-likelihood objective and yield analytically tractable inference; the CLT also motivates Normal approximations for aggregated random error.

items.question: How do you compute P(X = k) for Binomial(n, p)?
items.answer: Use the PMF P(X=k) = C(n,k) p^k (1-p)^(n-k) for k = 0,…,n, or library functions like scipy.stats.binom.pmf(k, n, p) for numerical evaluation.

items.question: Bernoulli vs Binomial: what changes?
items.answer: Bernoulli is one trial (X ∈ {0,1}); Binomial counts successes in n independent Bernoulli(p) trials (X ∈ {0,…,n}). Binomial with n=1 reduces to Bernoulli.

items.question: Poisson vs Binomial for rare events?
items.answer: Binomial(n,p) with small p and large n approximates Poisson(λ ≈ np); Poisson is simpler when n is unknown but a rate per interval is natural. Poisson requires mean = variance; Binomial allows Var = np(1-p) < np.

items.question: Categorical vs Bernoulli in classification?
items.answer: Bernoulli models one binary label (or one-vs-rest); Categorical models exactly one of K ≥ 2 classes with probabilities summing to 1. Multi-class softmax is Categorical; sigmoid on one output is Bernoulli.

items.question: Gaussian PDF vs discrete PMF for features?
items.answer: Gaussian PDF models continuous real measurements (height, sensor reading); PMFs model discrete counts or classes. GNB uses Gaussian PDFs per continuous feature; binary features often use Bernoulli likelihoods instead.
